content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against the fork point and ran `nix build` successfully. Found one correctness issue in the proposal threshold accounting.

## Amendments

1. `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1999` groups propose-new-bead amendments only by normalized proposal title, and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:2012` sums each finding's `occurrence_count` to decide whether the N=2 threshold is met. Because review-stage findings are assigned `occurrence_count: 1` at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1091`, a single reviewer/payload can emit two duplicate findings with the same proposed title and incorrectly satisfy the two-source creation threshold. The same double-counting can happen in final review if one source contributes multiple accepted amendments with the same proposed title. This violates the required "proposed by at least 2 sources" policy and can still create/propose beads from one noisy source. Carry a stable source identity per finding and threshold on distinct sources per proposal group instead of summed occurrences.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: The central proliferation guard is source quorum; counting duplicate findings as independent sources undermines the policy this bead is meant to enforce.
